PHASE 40 OBSERVABILITY RUNBOOK — Sentry live + UptimeRobot + cold-start
=======================================================================
Written 2026-07-21. Code side is done on branch feat/phase-40-observability
(pytest green incl. 4 new sentry-debug tests, tsc clean, lint at baseline,
tokenless prod build proven). Everything below the "CODE" section happens
in dashboards and is done by Cesar, except step 0 (agent measures it).

Statuses: [x] done  [ ] not yet run. Update this file as steps complete,
in the order they were actually run. Keep AS-RUN NOTES at the bottom.


CODE (done, on branch feat/phase-40-observability)
--------------------------------------------------
[x] settings.py Sentry init: send_default_pii=False, environment from
    SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT (default "production"), release from
    RENDER_GIT_COMMIT (Render injects it; local runs get no release)
[x] GET /api/sentry-debug/ raises ZeroDivisionError ONLY when
    SENTRY_DEBUG_ENDPOINT=true; otherwise 404. No auth. 4 guard tests.
[x] Frontend: reactRouterV7BrowserTracingIntegration in main.tsx,
    SentryRoutes wrapper in App.tsx, 5xx/network-only capture in the
    axios interceptor (never 401/403/404), replay masking left at
    defaults
[x] @sentry/vite-plugin in devDependencies; vite.config.ts adds it only
    when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is set; build.sourcemap = "hidden"; maps are
    deleted after upload so they never deploy
[x] Build without token verified: map generated locally, served JS has
    no sourceMappingURL comment


STEP 0 — COLD-START FIGURE (recorded BEFORE any monitor exists)
---------------------------------------------------------------
Once 5-minute keep-warm pings start this can never be measured again.
REORDERED: now runs after step 1's merge settles (see AS-RUN NOTES —
attempt 1 was polluted by the step-3 redeploy). Until the figure is
recorded here, nothing may touch the Render API: no step 6, no
browsing the live app (it calls the API). Steps 4-5 are Cloudflare-
only and safe to do during the wait.
[x] After >=15 min of API idle:
      cold: time curl -s https://stemquest-api.onrender.com/api/health/
      warm: run it again immediately
    RESULTS (2026-07-21): NO COLD START EXISTS TO MEASURE.
      attempt 1 (18 min idle):  0.170s   (assumed polluted, discarded)
      attempt 2 (~20 min idle): 0.157s   (assumed polluted, discarded)
      attempt 3 (45 min idle):  0.151s / warm 0.121s
    Three windows up to 45 minutes with zero traffic from us, and the
    service answered in ~0.15s every time — it is NOT spinning down.
    Likely causes: Render probing the healthCheckPath from render.yaml,
    and/or ambient internet-scanner traffic to the public
    *.onrender.com hostname. Either way, the long-feared free-tier
    cold start is empirically not occurring for this service, which
    also removes the keep-warm rationale for UptimeRobot (step 8 was
    descoped anyway). If Render's behavior ever changes, symptoms
    would be a ~30-60s first response after idle.


STEP 1 — MERGE (Cesar)
----------------------
[x] Open PR from feat/phase-40-observability into main, confirm CI is
    green, merge.  (PR #29, merged 2026-07-21, CI green)
    Note: merging redeploys both the Render API and the Cloudflare
    Worker. Neither has Sentry env vars yet, so both keep running with
    Sentry inert — that is fine; the env vars come next.


STEP 2 — SENTRY ACCOUNT + PROJECTS (Cesar, sentry.io)
-----------------------------------------------------
[x] Create account with cesarvillarreal11@gmail.com (free/Developer
    plan is enough). One org.
[x] Create project 1: platform Django, name stemquest-django
[x] Create project 2: platform React, name stemquest-react
[x] WRITE DOWN (do not paste into chat or commit anywhere):
      - DSN for stemquest-django   (Project > Settings > Client Keys)
      - DSN for stemquest-react
      - org slug        (in the URL: sentry.io/organizations/<org>/)
      - project slug    (stemquest-react — needed for source maps)
[x] Create an auth token for source-map upload:
    Settings > Developer Settings > Organization Tokens (or Auth Tokens)
    > create token with project:releases + org:read (the "source map
    upload" preset if offered). WRITE DOWN the token — it shows once.


STEP 3 — RENDER ENV VARS (Cesar, Render > stemquest-api > Environment)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
[x] SENTRY_DSN = <stemquest-django DSN from step 2>
    (SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT is optional — it already defaults to
    "production". RENDER_GIT_COMMIT is injected by Render automatically,
    nothing to set.)
    Saving env vars auto-redeploys (~2 min).
[x] Do NOT set SENTRY_DEBUG_ENDPOINT yet — that is step 6.


STEP 4 — CLOUDFLARE WORKERS BUILD VARIABLES (Cesar, Cloudflare dash)
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Workers & Pages > stemquest > Settings > BUILD > Variables.
SAME TRAP AS PHASE 39: these must be BUILD variables, not runtime
Worker variables — Vite and the Sentry plugin run at build time.
[ ] VITE_SENTRY_DSN    = <stemquest-react DSN from step 2>
[ ] SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN  = <token from step 2>   (mark as secret)
[ ] SENTRY_ORG         = <org slug>
[ ] SENTRY_PROJECT     = stemquest-react
[ ] Trigger a redeploy (Deployments > retry latest, or push to main).
    Build log should show the Sentry plugin uploading source maps.


STEP 5 — VERIFY SOURCE MAPS DID NOT DEPLOY (Cesar or agent)
-----------------------------------------------------------
[ ] View source of the live site, find the hashed JS asset URL, then:
      curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" <asset URL>.map
    Expect 404.
[ ] curl -s <asset URL> | tail -c 200   -> no sourceMappingURL=...map


STEP 6 — BACKEND SENTRY SMOKE TEST (Cesar, Render + sentry.io)
--------------------------------------------------------------
[ ] Render > Environment: add SENTRY_DEBUG_ENDPOINT = true (redeploys)
[ ] curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
      https://stemquest-api.onrender.com/api/sentry-debug/
    Expect 500.
[ ] In sentry.io > stemquest-django: a ZeroDivisionError event appears
    with environment=production and a release SHA. OPEN THE EVENT and
    confirm NO user email/IP is attached (PII check —
    send_default_pii=False doing its job).
[ ] Render > Environment: REMOVE SENTRY_DEBUG_ENDPOINT (redeploys)
[ ] curl the endpoint again -> 404


STEP 7 — FRONTEND SENTRY SMOKE TEST (Cesar, browser + sentry.io)
----------------------------------------------------------------
[ ] On https://stemquest.cesarvillarreal11.workers.dev/ open devtools
    console and run:
      setTimeout(() => { throw new Error('phase-40 smoke') })
    (setTimeout escapes the console's own error handling; Sentry's
    global handlers catch it — no code change needed.)
[ ] In sentry.io > stemquest-react: the event appears with
      - a READABLE stack trace pointing at real file names/lines
        (proves the source-map upload worked), and
      - the route name from router instrumentation.


STEP 8 — UPTIMEROBOT (Cesar, uptimerobot.com)  ** DESCOPED 2026-07-21 **
---------------------------------------------
Cesar decided not to implement UptimeRobot. Consequences accepted:
no down alerts, deep DB check unwatched, and Render cold starts
return for the first request after 15+ idle minutes. If keep-warm is
ever wanted without an UptimeRobot account: a GitHub Actions cron
curling /api/health/ every 10 min does the same job for free.
[ ] Create free account, confirm alert email address works.
[ ] Monitor 1 (keep-warm + uptime): HTTP(s),
      https://stemquest-api.onrender.com/api/health/
      interval 5 min.
    Shallow ON PURPOSE: it never touches the DB, so it keeps Render
    warm without keeping Neon's compute awake.
[ ] Monitor 2 (DB alerting): Keyword monitor,
      https://stemquest-api.onrender.com/api/health/?deep=1
      keyword: "database": "ok"   (alert when keyword NOT exists)
      interval 60 min. (~24 brief Neon wakes/day, negligible.)
[ ] Monitor 3 (frontend): HTTP(s),
      https://stemquest.cesarvillarreal11.workers.dev/
      interval 5 min.
[ ] Alert-email proof: create a scratch monitor pointing at
      https://stemquest-api.onrender.com/api/nope/
    wait for the down alert email to arrive, then DELETE the monitor.


STEP 9 — POST-SETUP CHECKS (Cesar, ~1 hour later)
-------------------------------------------------
[ ] All three monitors green; deep monitor shows the keyword match.
[ ] Keep-warm proof: after 20+ min of no manual traffic, hit the API
    once — response should be instant (compare against the cold figure
    from step 0).
[ ] Manual click-through of the live site: register/login, course map,
    open a lesson. Console clean; sentry.io shows NO noise events from
    normal browsing (no 4xx spam from the interceptor, sampling sane).
[ ] Optional cleanup while in /admin/: delete Phase 39 test account
    r2-check@example.com.


AS-RUN NOTES
------------
(append what actually happened, in order, including any missteps)

2026-07-21  Steps 2 and 3 were done BEFORE step 1 — the PR did not
            exist yet when Cesar started the dashboard work. Harmless,
            with two knock-ons:
            (a) prod briefly ran the OLD Sentry init (send_default_pii
                =True) once SENTRY_DSN was set; fixed by the merge.
            (b) the step-3 env-var save redeployed the API mid-way
                through the agent's 18-min idle window, so cold-start
                attempt 1 measured a warm service (0.17s — discarded).
            Step 0 re-armed: agent watches for the merge, waits ~25 min
            (deploy + 15 min idle), then measures.

2026-07-21  PR #29 merged (CI green). Phase closed early by choice:
            steps 4-7 deferred (frontend Sentry stays dormant — its
            env vars were never added to the Cloudflare build), step 8
            UptimeRobot DESCOPED, step 9 moot. Backend Sentry is LIVE
            but step 6's smoke test was not run — recommended before
            trusting the pipeline. Next session pivots to portfolio
            polish (README + jdoe@demo.com demo account); see the
            2026-07-21 phase-40 handoff.
